Commuter railroads will see an average increase of up to 4.5% to monthlies, weeklies, and one-way peak tickets. One-way passenger vehicle toll rates will increase between 20 cents to 60 cents at all bridge and tunnel facilities.

title: Adoption of Fare and Toll Increases and Policy Changes
description: The MTA Board approved toll and fare increases for New York City Transit subways and buses, Access-A-Ride, Long Island Rail Road (LIRR), and Metro-North Railroad, effective January 2026. This includes adjustments to the base fare for subways/local buses, reduced fares, and express bus fares. Policy changes include making OMNY seven-day fare-capping permanent, extending fare-capping to the express bus network, requiring tap-and-ride for fare payment on subways and buses by mid-2026 (discontinuing coin acceptance on buses), and changes to OMNY card fees. Additionally, one-way passenger-vehicle toll rates will increase at bridges and tunnels.
